compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is in a state of advanced decay. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable from debris. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, though severely compromised.

plant_audit.p1: No visible plant matter; pot appears empty.
plant_audit.p2: No visible plant matter; pot appears empty.
plant_audit.p3: Single leaf remaining, showing signs of chlorosis and turgor loss.
plant_audit.p4: No visible plant matter; pot appears empty.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears desiccated with significant debris (eggshell fragments) present.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in P3/P4 area; no signs of active growth or moss.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no biological debris outside of pots.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial observation shows minimal plant presence; P3 anchor is visible.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Progressive loss of structural integrity in the remaining P3 leaf.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Stagnation; no recovery observed. The biome is effectively dormant or dead.

visual_health_inference.p3: Critical. The specimen is likely undergoing terminal senescence due to lack of light or improper substrate moisture.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: Non-viable. The lack of foliage across the registry suggests a total collapse of the indoor botanical environment.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome has suffered a catastrophic loss of all specimens except for a single, failing Pothos leaf. The environment shows no signs of active growth, and the soil appears to have been abandoned or neglected for an extended period. The presence of eggshells suggests a failed intervention to restore soil nutrients.
